title: Getting started with regulog
text: regulog_init() creates session object. Every subsequent log call attached object. path omitted, log lives memory — suitable development testing. production, always supply path entries survive R session. genesis record written immediately regulog_init(). SHA-256 hash anchors entire chain — see vignette("hash-chain") cryptographic linking works.
code: log <- regulog_init(   app     = "primary-analysis",   version = "1.0.0",   user    = "ndoh.penn"   # Provide path = "logs/audit.rlog" in production for persistent storage )  log #> <regulog> #>   App:     primary-analysis v1.0.0 #>   User:    ndoh.penn #>   Entries: 0 #>   Path:    (in-memory only)

title: Getting started with regulog
text: log_action() records discrete event. reason argument mandatory default — undocumented entries rejected. action object fields accept strings — choose controlled vocabulary suits organisation. Common patterns:
code: log_action(log,   action = "data_read",   object = "adsl.sas7bdat",   reason = "Reading subject-level dataset for primary efficacy analysis" ) #> regulog: logged action 'data_read' on 'adsl.sas7bdat' # Analytical steps log_action(log,   action = "model_fit",   object = "primary_ANCOVA",   reason = "Fitting ANCOVA: CHG ~ TRT01P + BASE + SITEID per SAP section 6.1" ) #> regulog: logged action 'model_fit' on 'primary_ANCOVA'  # Data exports log_action(log,   action = "export",   object = "Table14_1.rtf",   reason = "Primary efficacy table exported for clinical study report" ) #> regulog: logged action 'export' on 'Table14_1.rtf'  # Review and approval events log_action(log,   action = "approved",   object = "primary_results_v3",   reason = "QC review complete — all outputs match SAP-specified formats" ) #> regulog: logged action 'approved' on 'primary_results_v3'  # User can override the session user for a single entry log_action(log,   action = "co_reviewed",   object = "primary_results_v3",   reason = "Independent statistical review complete",   user   = "second.reviewer" ) #> regulog: logged action 'co_reviewed' on 'primary_results_v3'

title: Getting started with regulog
text: log_change() captures /modification — primary mechanism satisfying 21 CFR Part 11 §11.10(e) change documentation. arguments coerced character, accept R value:
code: log_change(log,   object = "alpha",   field  = "value",   before = "0.05",   after  = "0.025",   reason = "Significance level updated per protocol amendment 2 (2026-05-01)" ) #> regulog: logged change to alpha$value # Data correction log_change(log,   object = "subject_01042",   field  = "ae_onset_date",   before = "2026-03-01",   after  = "2026-03-11",   reason = "Transcription error — corrected per source CRF page 47, query Q-0192" ) #> regulog: logged change to subject_01042$ae_onset_date  # Configuration update log_change(log,   object = "model_config",   field  = "covariance_structure",   before = "compound_symmetry",   after  = "unstructured",   reason = "Unstructured covariance pre-specified in SAP section 6.1.2" ) #> regulog: logged change to model_config$covariance_structure  # Population definition change log_change(log,   object = "analysis_population",   field  = "SAFFL_definition",   before = "RANDFL = 'Y'",   after  = "RANDFL = 'Y' AND EXOCCUR = 'Y'",   reason = "Protocol amendment 3: safety population requires confirmed dosing" ) #> regulog: logged change to analysis_population$SAFFL_definition

title: Getting started with regulog
text: log_note() captures free-text annotations — rationale, observation, decision fit discrete action verb /field change. Common uses:
code: # Outlier decision log_note(log,   "Outlier identified for subject 01-042 at Week 16 (AVAL = 98.4,    upper fence = 62.1). Discussed with medical monitor on 2026-06-20.    Retained in primary analysis per SAP section 8.3 — no protocol    deviation recorded. Sensitivity analysis without outlier pre-specified    in SAP section 10.4." ) #> regulog: note logged  # Protocol deviation log_note(log,   "Subject 01-007: visit window deviation at Week 8 (visited Day 61,    window Day 50-58). Classified as minor deviation per deviation    assessment log entry DEV-0031. Subject retained in ITT population." ) #> regulog: note logged  # Query resolved log_note(log,   "Data query Q-0047 resolved 2026-06-15: lab value for subject 01-019    at Screening confirmed as 4.2 mmol/L per site laboratory report.    Original value 42.0 was a decimal error." ) #> regulog: note logged  # Analysis assumption documented log_note(log,   "Missing baseline value for subject 01-033: LOCF imputation applied    per SAP section 7.2 — previous non-missing value (Visit 1) used.    Imputed value: 24.6." ) #> regulog: note logged

title: Getting started with regulog
text: Manually calling log_action() every file read error-prone. with_log() patches common read functions automatically duration code block, restores exit — even block errors. Functions patched automatically package loaded: haven::read_sas, haven::read_xpt, readr::read_csv, data.table::fread, utils::read.csv, utils::read.table. auto-logged entry captures file path, row count, column count. example: persistent hooks across multiple steps, use log_hooks_enable() log_hooks_disable(): with_log() preferred manual pair guarantees log_hooks_disable() called via .exit() even error occurs.
code: with_log(log, {   adsl <- haven::read_sas("data/adsl.sas7bdat")   # logged automatically   adae <- haven::read_sas("data/adae.sas7bdat")   # logged automatically   adlb <- haven::read_sas("data/adlb.sas7bdat")   # logged automatically   params <- readr::read_csv("config/parameters.csv") # logged automatically }) action: data_read object: data/adsl.sas7bdat reason: haven::read_sas("data/adsl.sas7bdat") — 298 rows, 47 cols log_hooks_enable(log)  adsl <- haven::read_sas("data/adsl.sas7bdat") adae <- haven::read_sas("data/adae.sas7bdat") # ... more reads ...  log_hooks_disable()  # always call this; or use with_log() for safety

title: Getting started with regulog
text: log_signature() records named, dated, meaningful sign-. Two things happen automatically — user input required: Signer identity resolved session user set regulog_init() — overridden signing time Entries covered captured count prior entries session moment signing Multiple signatures supported — example, lead statistician independent reviewer:
code: log_signature(log,   "I certify that this primary analysis is accurate and complete,    conducted in accordance with SAP version 2.0 dated 2026-05-01" ) #> regulog: signature applied by 'ndoh.penn' covering 13 entries log_signature(log,   "Statistical analysis complete and accurate per SAP v2.0.    All deviations documented." )  # Second reviewer — create a new log or log against the same path with # a different session user log2 <- regulog_init(app = "primary-analysis", version = "1.0.0",                       user = "second.reviewer",                       path = "logs/trial001_audit.rlog")  log_signature(log2,   "Independent QC review complete. Results independently verified." )

title: Getting started with regulog
text: verify_log() recomputes every entry hash confirms prev_hash links correctly predecessor. Works live regulog object .rlog file path. return value carries structured results: Tampering reliably detected: Verification file path requires live session:
code:
verify_log(log) #> regulog: Log intact: 14 entries, chain unbroken result <- verify_log(log, verbose = FALSE) cat("Intact:        ", result$intact,       "
") #> Intact:         TRUE cat("Entries checked:", result$n_entries,    "
") #> Entries checked: 14 cat("First broken:  ", result$first_broken,  "
") #> First broken:   NA saved <- log$entries[[2L]]$reason log$entries[[2L]]$reason <- "ALTERED REASON"  tamper_result <- suppressWarnings(verify_log(log, verbose = FALSE)) cat("Intact after tamper:", tamper_result$intact,        "
") #> Intact after tamper: FALSE cat("First broken entry: ", tamper_result$first_broken,  "
") #> First broken entry:  2  log$entries[[2L]]$reason <- saved  # restore verify_log("logs/trial001_audit.rlog")

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: regulog_init() called, genesis record created immediately. log entry usual sense — carries user action — SHA-256 hash becomes anchor entire chain. genesis hash incorporates app name, version, creation timestamp. Two sessions app version different creation times different genesis hashes.
code: log <- regulog_init(app = "demo", version = "1.0", user = "analyst")  cat("Genesis hash:", log$genesis_hash, "\n") #> Genesis hash: 138d8d12317e0b47caa64a546d1da6f8db89dab78c49eba3bb8c1ff3371aca5f cat("Last hash:   ", log$last_hash,    "\n") #> Last hash:    138d8d12317e0b47caa64a546d1da6f8db89dab78c49eba3bb8c1ff3371aca5f

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: entry hash computed canonical string includes every field entry plus hash previous entry: pipe character | delimiter. Field values concatenated fixed, deterministic order — makes hash reproducible verification. Let us look looks like practice: prev_hash first entry matches genesis_hash. chain begun. entry’s hash prev_hash next. chain intact.
code:
h_n = SHA256(   entry_id | timestamp | app | app_version | user | type |   <payload fields in sorted key order> | h_{n-1} ) log_action(log, "approved", "dataset_v1",            "All quality checks passed — dataset approved for analysis") #> regulog: logged action 'approved' on 'dataset_v1'  entry <- log$entries[[1L]] cat("Entry ID:   ", entry$entry_id,   "
") #> Entry ID:    1 cat("Prev hash:  ", entry$prev_hash,  "
")  # = genesis hash #> Prev hash:   138d8d12317e0b47caa64a546d1da6f8db89dab78c49eba3bb8c1ff3371aca5f cat("Entry hash: ", entry$entry_hash, "
") #> Entry hash:  e0fc7a480c7a5c9e925070ee20ff88d2ed38bc5d287dc387fa1d1ca5414da26c log_action(log, "model_fit", "ANCOVA_v1", "Primary ANCOVA fitted per SAP") #> regulog: logged action 'model_fit' on 'ANCOVA_v1' log_note(log,   "Outlier in subject 042 retained per SAP section 8.3") #> regulog: note logged  cat("Entry 1 hash:", log$entries[[1L]]$entry_hash, "
") #> Entry 1 hash: e0fc7a480c7a5c9e925070ee20ff88d2ed38bc5d287dc387fa1d1ca5414da26c cat("Entry 2 prev:", log$entries[[2L]]$prev_hash,  "
") #> Entry 2 prev: e0fc7a480c7a5c9e925070ee20ff88d2ed38bc5d287dc387fa1d1ca5414da26c cat("Match:       ", log$entries[[1L]]$entry_hash ==                      log$entries[[2L]]$prev_hash,   "
") #> Match:        TRUE

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: verify_log() recomputes every entry hash scratch checks : recomputed hash matches stored entry_hash — confirms entry content modified stored prev_hash matches preceding entry’s entry_hash — confirms entries inserted, deleted, reordered return value always list structured fields:
code: verify_log(log) #> regulog: Log intact: 3 entries, chain unbroken result <- verify_log(log, verbose = FALSE) str(result) #> List of 4 #>  $ intact      : logi TRUE #>  $ n_entries   : int 3 #>  $ first_broken: int NA #>  $ errors      : chr(0)

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: field — reason, action, timestamp, user — changed writing, recomputed hash match stored entry_hash. entry fails step 1 verification.
code:
# Simulate a reason being altered original_reason <- log$entries[[1L]]$reason log$entries[[1L]]$reason <- "ALTERED"  result <- suppressWarnings(verify_log(log, verbose = FALSE)) cat("Intact:       ", result$intact,       "
") #> Intact:        FALSE cat("First broken: ", result$first_broken,  "
") #> First broken:  1 cat("Error:        ", result$errors[[1L]],  "
") #> Error:         Entry #1: entry_hash mismatch — content may have been modified  log$entries[[1L]]$reason <- original_reason  # restore

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: entry 2 deleted, entry 3’s prev_hash longer match entry 1’s entry_hash. chain fails step 2 entry 3.
code: saved <- log$entries log$entries <- log$entries[-2L]   # remove entry 2  result <- suppressWarnings(verify_log(log, verbose = FALSE)) cat("Intact:       ", result$intact,       "\n") #> Intact:        FALSE cat("First broken: ", result$first_broken,  "\n") #> First broken:  3  log$entries <- saved  # restore

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
code: saved_prev <- log$entries[[2L]]$prev_hash log$entries[[2L]]$prev_hash <- paste(rep("0", 64L), collapse = "")  result <- suppressWarnings(verify_log(log, verbose = FALSE)) cat("Intact:       ", result$intact, "\n") #> Intact:        FALSE cat("First broken: ", result$first_broken, "\n") #> First broken:  2  log$entries[[2L]]$prev_hash <- saved_prev  # restore verify_log(log, verbose = FALSE)$intact    # confirm restored #> [1] TRUE

what: 5. What the chain does NOT protect against
title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: hash chain proves entries modified writing. : Authenticate user — user string; regulog verify person set user = "ndoh.penn" actually person. Authentication responsibility calling system (OS login, Shiny Server Pro, Posit Connect). Prevent future entries — anyone write access .rlog file can append new entries. chain covers written; prevent additions. Encrypt content — .rlog files plain JSON. store sensitive data log entries; store references (file names, IDs) instead. Protect file deletion — .rlog file deleted, audit trail gone. Use standard file system controls backups.

what: 6. Verifying a file without a live session
title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: .rlog file self-contained. Verification require original regulog object — works directly file path: intended QC workflow regulated environments: analyst runs analysis produces .rlog, reviewer verifies file independently.
code: # Can be run by a QC reviewer with no knowledge of the original session result <- verify_log("logs/trial001_audit.rlog")  # Structured result for programmatic use if (!result$intact) {   warning(sprintf(     "Log integrity failure: %d error(s). First broken entry: #%d",     length(result$errors), result$first_broken   )) }

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: path supplied regulog_init(), entries written disk immediately — log_action(), log_change(), etc. appends one JSON line. file append-regulog’s perspective. genesis record always line 1. Subsequent entries follow order. line complete, self-contained JSON object.
code: log <- regulog_init(   app     = "trial-analysis",   version = "1.0.0",   user    = "ndoh.penn",   path    = "logs/trial001_audit.rlog" )  log_action(log, "data_read", "adsl.sas7bdat", "Reading ADSL") # ↑ This line is written to disk immediately  # The .rlog file at this point: # Line 1: {"entry_id":0,"type":"GENESIS",...} # Line 2: {"entry_id":1,"type":"ACTION","action":"data_read",...}

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works
text: .rlog files newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON). line one entry: format chosen deliberately: Human-readable without specialist software Streamable — tools like jq can filter entries without loading whole file Append-safe — file rewriting needed; entry one line Portable — plain text, binary encoding
code: {"entry_id":0,"timestamp":"2026-06-23T10:00:00.000Z","app":"trial-analysis","app_version":"1.0.0","user":"ndoh.penn","type":"GENESIS","prev_hash":"0","entry_hash":"a3f8c2..."} {"entry_id":1,"timestamp":"2026-06-23T10:01:22.841Z","app":"trial-analysis","app_version":"1.0.0","user":"ndoh.penn","type":"ACTION","action":"data_read","object":"adsl.sas7bdat","reason":"Reading ADSL","prev_hash":"a3f8c2...","entry_hash":"b7d94e..."}

title: The hash chain: how tamper detection works

title: Shiny integration
text: Call regulog_shiny_init() top server(). Store returned log object local variable pass log_* calls inside observers.
code: library(shiny) library(regulog)  server <- function(input, output, session) {    log <- regulog_shiny_init(     session = session,     app     = "clinical-review-tool",     version = "1.2.0",     path    = "logs/review_audit.rlog"   )    observeEvent(input$approve, {     req(nzchar(input$justification))     log_action(log,       action = "approved",       object = input$dataset_id,       reason = input$justification     )     showNotification("Approval recorded.", type = "message")   })    observeEvent(input$reject, {     req(nzchar(input$rejection_reason))     log_action(log,       action = "rejected",       object = input$dataset_id,       reason = input$rejection_reason     )     showNotification("Rejection recorded.", type = "warning")   }) }

title: Shiny integration
text: regulog_observer() reduces boilerplate many UI events need auditing. object reason arguments accept fixed strings reactive expressions.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {    log <- regulog_shiny_init(     session = session,     app     = "data-review",     version = "2.0.0",     path    = "logs/review.rlog"   )    # Static strings   regulog_observer(log, session,     eventExpr = input$lock_database,     action    = "database_locked",     object    = "study_database",     reason    = "Database lock confirmed by data manager"   )    # Reactive strings — evaluated at the time the event fires   regulog_observer(log, session,     eventExpr = input$approve_record,     action    = "approved",     object    = reactive(paste0("record_", input$record_id)),     reason    = reactive(input$approval_reason)   )    regulog_observer(log, session,     eventExpr = input$flag_discrepancy,     action    = "flagged",     object    = reactive(paste0("record_", input$record_id)),     reason    = reactive(paste0("Discrepancy: ", input$discrepancy_detail))   ) }

code: # In global.R — outside server(), evaluated once at startup shared_log <- regulog_init(   app     = "multi-user-review",   version = "1.0.0",   user    = "system",             # will be overridden per action   path    = "logs/shared_audit.rlog" )  server <- function(input, output, session) {    # Resolve per-request user   current_user <- reactive({     u <- session$user     if (is.null(u) || !nzchar(u)) Sys.info()[["user"]] else u   })    observeEvent(input$approve, {     req(nzchar(input$reason))     log_action(shared_log,       action = "approved",       object = input$record_id,       reason = input$reason,       user   = current_user()   # explicit per-action user     )   }) }

function(input, output, session) {    log <- regulog_shiny_init(     session = session,     app     = "data-review",     version = "1.0.0",     path    = tempfile(fileext = ".rlog")   )    observeEvent(input$approve, {     req(nzchar(input$justification))     log_action(log, "approved", input$dataset_id, input$justification)     showNotification("Approval recorded.", type = "message")   })    observeEvent(input$reject, {     req(nzchar(input$justification))     log_action(log, "rejected", input$dataset_id, input$justification)     showNotification("Rejection recorded.", type = "warning")   })    observeEvent(input$add_note, {     req(nzchar(input$note_text))     log_note(log, input$note_text)     showNotification("Note recorded.", type = "message")   }) c("entry_id", "type", "action", "object", "reason")]   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging for R
text: Tamper-Evident Audit Logging R Every analytical action taken consequential R environment documented — , , , . practice, almost none . regulog fills gap. records every action, change, note, decision tamper-evident, hash-chained audit trail stored newline-delimited JSON. Every entry attributed named user, time-stamped UTC, linked previous entry via SHA-256 — modification fact, however subtle, detectable verify_log(). Works regulated pharmaceutical environments (21 CFR Part 11, EU Annex 11), internal data pipelines, multi-user Shiny applications, context accountability traceability matter.

code: library(regulog)  # Initialise a session log <- regulog_init(   app     = "primary-analysis",   version = "1.0.0",   user    = "analyst",   path    = "logs/audit.rlog" )  # Log actions, changes, and decisions log_action(log, "data_read", "adsl.sas7bdat",            "Reading ADSL for primary efficacy analysis")  log_change(log, object = "alpha", field = "value",            before = "0.05", after = "0.025",            reason = "Updated per protocol amendment 2")  log_note(log,   "Outlier in subject 01-042 retained per SAP section 8.3 —    discussed with medical monitor 2026-06-20")  # Automate data I/O logging — no code changes needed with_log(log, {   adsl <- haven::read_sas("data/adsl.sas7bdat")   # auto-logged   adae <- haven::read_sas("data/adae.sas7bdat")   # auto-logged })  # Apply an electronic signature log_signature(log,   "I certify this analysis is accurate and complete per SAP version 2.0")  # Verify tamper integrity verify_log(log) #> regulog: Log intact: 5 entries, chain unbroken  # Query the log filter_log(log, type = "SIGNATURE") filter_log(log, action = "data_read", from = "2026-06-01")  # Export for submission export_audit_trail(log, format = "csv", signed = TRUE,                    path = "outputs/audit_trail.csv")

code: log <- regulog_init(   app     = "primary-analysis",   version = "1.0.0",   user    = "ndoh.penn",   path    = "logs/trial001_audit.rlog" ) log_action(log, "data_read", "adsl.sas7bdat",            "Reading ADSL for primary efficacy analysis")  log_change(log, object = "param_alpha", field = "value",            before = "0.05", after = "0.025",            reason = "Updated per protocol amendment 2 (2026-05-01)")  log_note(log, "Outlier in subject 042 at Week 16 retained per SAP               section 8.3 after discussion with medical monitor") # Scoped: hooks on for the block, guaranteed off on exit with_log(log, {   adsl <- haven::read_sas("data/adsl.sas7bdat")   # auto-logged   adae <- haven::read_sas("data/adae.sas7bdat")   # auto-logged })  # Manual: enable then disable explicitly log_hooks_enable(log) adlb <- haven::read_sas("data/adlb.sas7bdat") log_hooks_disable() log_signature(log,   "I certify that this analysis is accurate and complete, conducted    in accordance with SAP version 2.0 dated 2026-05-01" ) # Verify tamper integrity verify_log(log)  # Query entries filter_log(log, type = "SIGNATURE") filter_log(log, action = "data_read", from = "2026-06-01")  # Export export_audit_trail(log, format = "csv", signed = TRUE,                    path = "outputs/audit_trail_TRIAL001.csv")

text: log regulog object character path .rlog file. verbose Logical. Print summary. Defaults TRUE.

title: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
text: list (invisibly) components: intact Logical. TRUE chain unbroken. n_entries Integer. Number data entries verified (genesis excluded). first_broken Integer NA. entry_id first invalid entry. errors Character vector error descriptions.

title: Verify the integrity of an audit log chain — verify_log
text: entry (excluding genesis record): Reconstruct hash_input stored fields canonical order. Recompute digest(hash_input, algo = hash_algo). Assert computed == entry$entry_hash (content integrity). Assert entry$prev_hash == entry[-1]$entry_hash (chain continuity). Step 3 failure: entry's content modified writing. Step 4 failure: entries inserted, deleted, reordered.

title: regulog 0.2.0
text: log_note() — log free-text annotation analytical decision tamper-evident NOTE entry. Every entry mandatory-reason enforced included hash chain. log_signature() — apply electronic signature per 21 CFR Part 11 §11.100/§11.200. Signer identity resolved session user; entries covered captured automatically. filter_log() — query log entries data.frame type, user, action, date range. Also works directly .rlog file paths without active session. .data.frame.regulog() — S3 method convert regulog object flat data frame, one row per entry (genesis record excluded). log_hooks_enable() / log_hooks_disable() — patch common read functions (haven::read_sas, readr::read_csv, data.table::fread, utils::read.csv, etc.) automatic data /O logging. Every read captured ACTION entry without code changes. with_log() — scoped automatic logging code block. Guarantees log_hooks_disable() called exit even block errors.
